proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 (a continuous feature ranging 30-90, plausibly age in years) is monotonically associated with pfs_months in this cohort.
feature_005 (an ordinal feature with three levels 0/1/2) shows a monotonic dose-response with pfs_months: higher feature_005 levels are associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.pearsonr(df.feature_012, df.pfs_months); stats.spearmanr(df.feature_012, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: feature_012 has a very strong positive correlation with pfs_months: Pearson r=+0.7015, Spearman rho=+0.7264 (both p<1e-300). Each SD (~9.95 units) of feature_012 is associated with +1.75 mo PFS in a multivariable OLS model.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df.feature_005==v,'pfs_months'] for v in [0,1,2]])
analyses.result_summary: feature_005 shows a clean monotonic dose-response in pfs_months: level 0 mean=5.64, level 1 mean=4.44, level 2 mean=3.29 (one-way ANOVA F=2860.75, p<1e-300). Multivariable OLS slope = -1.17 mo per level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features, feature_006=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_006=0.
feature_001=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_001=0 (likely an unfavorable prognostic biomarker).
feature_013=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_013=0, consistent with a treatment-like positive effect.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_006==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_006==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=1 mean pfs=3.617 vs feature_006=0 mean pfs=5.153, diff=-1.536 mo (Welch t-test p<1e-300). Largest negative binary main effect in the dataset.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_001==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_001==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: feature_001=1 (10% prevalence) mean pfs=3.750 vs feature_001=0 mean=4.800, diff=-1.050 mo (Welch t-test p=1.45e-178).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_013==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_013==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 (35% prevalence) mean pfs=5.408 vs feature_013=0 mean=4.310, diff=+1.098 mo (Welch t-test p<1e-300). Largest positive binary main effect; consistent with feature_013 being a treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_029=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_029=0 in the cohort overall.
feature_028=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_028=0 in the cohort overall.
feature_018=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_018=0 in the cohort overall.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_029==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_029==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: feature_029=1 (70% prevalence) mean pfs=4.865 vs feature_029=0 mean=4.297, diff=+0.568 mo (Welch t-test p=3.7e-143).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_028==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_028==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: feature_028=1 (35% prevalence) mean pfs=4.328 vs feature_028=0 mean=4.887, diff=-0.559 mo (Welch t-test p=6.1e-144).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_018==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_018==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: feature_018=1 (18% prevalence) mean pfs=4.317 vs feature_018=0 mean=4.776, diff=-0.459 mo (Welch t-test p=6.1e-70).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous features, feature_004 is negatively correlated with pfs_months and feature_024 is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_033 (continuous, range 1-100) has a negative association with pfs_months in the overall cohort.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df.feature_004, df.pfs_months); stats.pearsonr(df.feature_024, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: feature_004 vs pfs_months: Pearson r=-0.1155 (p=4.07e-148). feature_024 vs pfs_months: Pearson r=+0.1002 (p=9.4e-112). Both directions match the hypothesis.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df.feature_033, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: feature_033 vs pfs_months overall: Pearson r=-0.0914 (p=2.98e-93). However, in the control arm (feature_013=0) the correlation is nearly zero (r=-0.002, p=0.68), suggesting the overall association is driven entirely by treatment-effect modification rather than a prognostic main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS model including all 37 features explains a substantial share (>50%) of the variance in pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, feature_023 (univariate +0.36 mo) and feature_016 (univariate +0.11 mo) lose their main effects, indicating they are not independent prognostic factors.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(df.pfs_months, sm.add_constant(df[features])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS with all 37 features yields R^2=0.792 (Adj R^2=0.792, F=5152, p<<0.001). The largest independent effects (per SD or per unit) are: feature_012 +1.75, feature_006 -1.55, feature_005 -1.17, feature_013 +1.10, feature_001 -0.98, feature_028 -0.58, feature_029 +0.53, feature_018 -0.47, feature_004 -0.30, feature_033 -0.23, feature_024 +0.24.

analyses.code: OLS coefficients for feature_023 and feature_016 in full multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: feature_023 multivariable coef = +0.014 (p=0.32, NS) and feature_016 multivariable coef = +0.002 (p=0.86, NS), confirming both lose their univariate associations after adjustment. feature_023 is highly correlated with feature_029 (r=0.669), explaining its univariate signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_013 on pfs_months is heterogeneous across patient subgroups; specifically, feature_013 has a much larger effect when feature_029=1 than when feature_029=0.
feature_013 effect is suppressed (no benefit) in patients with feature_028=1.
feature_013 effect is suppressed (no benefit) in patients with feature_018=1.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_013 + feature_029 + feature_013:feature_029; stratified means
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 x feature_029 interaction coefficient = +1.594 (p=9.3e-229). Stratified: in feature_029=0 (n=15114), feature_013 effect = -0.018 mo (p=0.63, NS); in feature_029=1 (n=34886), feature_013 effect = +1.577 mo (p<1e-300). Treatment effect is essentially confined to feature_029=1.

analyses.code: Stratified mean comparison and OLS interaction
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 x feature_028 interaction = -1.623 (p=4.0e-255). Stratified: in feature_028=0 (n=32635), feature_013 effect = +1.659 mo (p<1e-300); in feature_028=1 (n=17365), feature_013 effect = +0.036 mo (p=0.29, NS). feature_028=1 fully eliminates the treatment benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified mean comparison and OLS interaction
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 x feature_018 interaction = -1.364 (p=2.2e-118). Stratified: in feature_018=0 (n=40940), feature_013 effect = +1.350 mo (p<1e-300); in feature_018=1 (n=9060), feature_013 effect = -0.014 mo (p=0.77, NS). feature_018=1 also fully suppresses the treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028, feature_018, and feature_029 have NO main prognostic effect on pfs_months in untreated (feature_013=0) patients; their apparent main effects in pooled analyses are entirely driven by being treatment-effect modifiers of feature_013.
feature_023 has no independent prognostic effect in feature_013=0 patients but enhances feature_013 efficacy.

analyses.code: Within feature_013=0: t-tests of pfs_months by each candidate modifier
analyses.result_summary: Within control arm (feature_013=0, n=32572): feature_028=1 vs 0 diff=+0.008 mo (p=0.74, NS); feature_018=1 vs 0 diff=+0.015 mo (p=0.64, NS); feature_029=1 vs 0 diff=+0.011 mo (p=0.68, NS). All three are pure treatment-effect modifiers with zero prognostic main effect.

analyses.code: Within feature_013=0: t-test by feature_023; within feature_013=1: t-test by feature_023
analyses.result_summary: Within control arm, feature_023=1 vs 0 diff=-0.020 mo (p=0.41, NS). Within treated arm (feature_013=1), feature_023=1 vs 0 diff=+1.072 mo (p=5.4e-134). Confirms feature_023 is a pure treatment-effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous biomarker feature_033 progressively suppress the feature_013 treatment effect on pfs_months, with effective benefit lost above approximately feature_033 = 15.

analyses.code: Within (feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0): bin feature_033 and report stratified treatment effects
analyses.result_summary: Within the favorable subgroup (feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0), the feature_013 effect by feature_033 bin is: <=5: +4.91 mo; 5-8: +4.98; 8-10: +4.99; 10-12: +4.92; 12-15: +3.50; 15-18: +0.26; 18-20: -0.20; 20-25: -0.26; 25+: ~+0.1. Treatment effect is large and stable below feature_033~12, drops sharply between 12-18, and is null above ~18. Continuous feature_013 x feature_033 interaction (z-scored) coef = -0.637 (p=1.5e-174).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup defining feature_013 efficacy is: feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033 <= ~12. Within this subgroup, feature_013 increases mean pfs_months by approximately +5 months; outside it, the effect is essentially zero.

Within the favorable subgroup defined in h19, the magnitude of the feature_013 treatment effect does NOT differ meaningfully across feature_005 levels (stage-like), feature_012 tertiles (age-like), or other prognostic features (feature_006, feature_001).

analyses.code: mask = (feature_029==1) & (feature_028==0) & (feature_018==0) & (feature_033<=12); ttest_ind on mask vs ~mask
analyses.result_summary: Inside the favorable subgroup (n=9206, 18.4% of cohort): feature_013=0 mean=4.280, feature_013=1 mean=9.235, diff=+4.955 mo (p<1e-300). Outside the favorable subgroup (n=40794, 81.6% of cohort): feature_013=0 mean=4.317, feature_013=1 mean=4.533, diff=+0.217 mo (p=2.9e-20; small residual likely from feature_033 bins 12-18). The favorable subgroup contains essentially all of the treatment benefit.

analyses.code: Within favorable subgroup, stratify by feature_005 and by feature_012 age-tertile
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup, feature_013 treatment effect by feature_005 level: 0 -> +5.01 mo, 1 -> +4.92 mo, 2 -> +5.00 mo. By feature_012 age-tertile: young -> +4.87 mo, mid -> +4.94 mo, old -> +5.11 mo. The treatment effect inside the favorable subgroup is uniform in magnitude across stage and age strata, even though those features strongly modulate baseline pfs.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous feature_012 (apparent age in years) has a strong, monotonic, near-linear positive association with pfs_months that is consistent across treatment status (i.e., it is a pure prognostic factor, not a treatment-effect modifier).
feature_006 and feature_001 are pure unfavorable prognostic factors with main effects but NO interaction with feature_013 treatment.

analyses.code: Within feature_013=0: stats.pearsonr(feature_012, pfs_months); cut feature_012 into bins and report mean pfs
analyses.result_summary: In the control arm (feature_013=0, n=32572) feature_012 vs pfs_months: Pearson r=+0.829 (p<1e-300). Mean pfs by feature_012 bin (control only): 20-40 -> 0.21, 40-50 -> 1.14, 50-55 -> 2.10, 55-60 -> 3.01, 60-65 -> 3.84, 65-70 -> 4.76, 70-75 -> 5.61, 75-80 -> 6.51, 80-90 -> 7.81. Near-linear positive relationship, ~0.13 mo per year of feature_012.

analyses.code: OLS interaction tests feature_013 x feature_006 and feature_013 x feature_001
analyses.result_summary: Stratified feature_013 effects: feature_006=0 -> +1.10 mo, feature_006=1 -> +1.10 mo (no interaction). feature_001=0 -> +1.10 mo, feature_001=1 -> +1.06 mo (no interaction). feature_006 and feature_001 are pure prognostic features that do not modify the feature_013 treatment effect; their effects are additive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final treatment-effect heterogeneity statement for pfs_months: feature_013 is a treatment whose efficacy is fully concentrated in patients with feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033 <= ~12; patients in this subgroup gain approximately +5 months of pfs from feature_013, while patients outside this subgroup gain approximately 0 months.

analyses.code: Final composite subgroup test with cutoff search for feature_033 in {10,12,13,14,15,16,17,18,20}
analyses.result_summary: Cutoff search for feature_033 within (feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0): cutoff=10 -> +4.96 mo (n=7362); cutoff=12 -> +4.95 (n=9206); cutoff=14 -> +4.94 (n=10828); cutoff=15 -> +4.65 (n=11575); cutoff=18 -> +4.09 (n=13326); cutoff=20 -> +3.83 (n=14226). Treatment benefit is stable up to ~feature_033=14 then attenuates. Recommended subgroup definition: feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0 & feature_033<=12 (effect +4.95 mo, p<1e-300, captures ~18% of cohort and ~92% of total absolute treatment benefit).
